name: API Layer
description: FastAPI application entry point that exposes REST endpoints for document ingestion, clinical querying, record listing, and deletion — the single HTTP surface through which external clients interact with the RAG system.

name: RAG Pipeline
description: Core retrieval-augmented generation modules responsible for orchestrating queries through intent parsing, strategy routing, cross-encoder reranking, LLM invocation, and answer synthesis — including deprecated stubs and package initializers for the rag module.

name: Data Layer
description: All data storage, retrieval, and persistence concerns: PostgreSQL schema management and CRUD operations, ChromaDB vector store with hybrid BM25+dense search, document ingestion pipeline, ICD-9 lookup table, SQL-based entity retrieval, database migration scripts, and sample patient/provider fixture data driving the clinical RAG system.

name: Service & Admin Layer
description: Cross-cutting operational services including HIPAA-compliant audit logging that records every query event to a rotating JSON log, and the CLI catalog-admin tool that manages the dynamic data-stream catalog powering the text-to-SQL query layer.

name: Frontend Layer
description: Streamlit chat interface that allows clinicians to query patient records, billing, and provider information in plain English, together with its Docker build definition and Python dependency manifest.

name: Infrastructure Layer
description: Docker Compose orchestration and container build definitions that assemble the four-service deployment topology: PostgreSQL, ChromaDB, FastAPI backend, and Streamlit frontend into a single locally-run, privacy-preserving stack.

name: Configuration Layer
description: Environment-driven configuration for the entire system: the central Pydantic Settings module that aggregates all runtime parameters, the environment variable template, and tooling configuration files for the Understand-Anything analysis plugin.

name: Documentation
description: Project documentation including the architecture overview and privacy rationale in the README, Docker deployment setup guide, development task tracker, and Python dependency manifests for the backend and frontend services.
